title: GitHub Copilot Configuration
description: Generate repository-wide instructions, path-specific rules, agent guidance, and settings for GitHub Copilot.

steps.title: Repository-Wide Instructions
steps.description: Configure .github/copilot-instructions.md — applied automatically to every Copilot interaction in this repository.
steps.hint: This is the most impactful Copilot config file. It is loaded automatically into all supported Copilot surfaces (Chat, completions, agents). Include coding standards, your stack, build/test commands, and conventions you always want enforced. Whitespace between instructions is ignored.

fields.label: File Heading
fields.description: The top-level heading written at the top of the file.

fields.label: Coding Standards & Conventions
fields.description: Style rules, naming conventions, and patterns Copilot should consistently follow.
fields.screen_hint: The best-practice defaults below are always included. Use this field to add project-specific conventions on top of them.
fields.locked_value:
- Validate all external input at system boundaries. Never trust data from users, APIs, or files.
- Never hardcode credentials, API keys, tokens, or secrets in source code.
- Handle errors explicitly. Never silently swallow exceptions.
- Prefer existing patterns over introducing new abstractions — consistency over novelty.
- Keep changes small and targeted to the stated task.
- Each unit of code should have a single, clear responsibility.
- Use descriptive, intention-revealing names for variables, functions, and types.

presets.label: TypeScript strict mode
presets.value: - Use TypeScript strict mode. Prefer explicit types at all public API boundaries.

presets.label: No `any` types
presets.description: Use `unknown` and narrow with type guards
presets.value: - Never use `any` — use `unknown` and narrow with type guards.

presets.label: Named exports only
presets.description: Avoid default exports for better refactoring
presets.value: - Use named exports exclusively. Avoid default exports.

presets.label: Async/await only
presets.value: - Use async/await throughout. Never use .then() chains or callbacks.

presets.label: Immutability first
presets.value: - Prefer immutability. Use const and readonly; avoid in-place mutation where possible.

presets.label: Error handling
presets.value: - Handle errors explicitly. Never silently swallow exceptions.

presets.label: Small focused changes
presets.description: One concern per PR or commit
presets.value: - Keep changes small and focused — one concern per PR.

presets.label: No magic numbers
presets.value: - Extract magic numbers and strings into named constants.

fields.label: Build & Test Commands
fields.description: The exact commands Copilot should suggest or run to build, test, and lint the project.
fields.placeholder:
- Install: npm install
- Build: npm run build
- Test: npm test
- Lint: npm run lint
- Type check: npm run typecheck
fields.screen_hint: Include every command needed to go from a fresh clone to a passing test run. Click a preset to load a common stack.

presets.label: npm / TypeScript
presets.value:
- Install: npm install
- Build: npm run build
- Test: npm test
- Lint: npm run lint
- Type check: npm run typecheck

presets.label: pnpm / TypeScript
presets.value:
- Install: pnpm install
- Build: pnpm build
- Test: pnpm test
- Lint: pnpm lint

presets.label: Python / FastAPI
presets.value:
- Install: pip install -r requirements.txt
- Run: python3 -m uvicorn app.main:app --reload
- Test: python3 -m pytest
- Type check: python3 -m mypy .

presets.label: FastAPI + React (monorepo)
presets.value:
- Both: make dev
- Backend only: make backend
- Frontend only: make frontend
- Install all: make install

fields.label: Architecture & Stack Notes
fields.description: Key architecture decisions, stack choices, and design patterns Copilot should respect.
fields.placeholder:
- Frontend: React 19 + Vite + TypeScript
- Backend: Python 3.13 + FastAPI + Pydantic v2
- State management: React hooks (no Redux or Zustand)
- Styling: Tailwind CSS v4
fields.screen_hint: Describe your stack so Copilot recommends patterns that fit your architecture. Click a preset to load a common stack description.

presets.label: React + Vite + TypeScript
presets.value:
- Frontend: React 19 + Vite + TypeScript
- Styling: Tailwind CSS v4
- State: React hooks — no Redux or Zustand

presets.label: FastAPI + Pydantic
presets.value:
- Backend: Python 3.13 + FastAPI + Pydantic v2
- ORM: SQLAlchemy (async)
- Auth: JWT tokens

presets.label: Next.js full-stack
presets.value:
- Framework: Next.js 15 App Router
- Language: TypeScript strict mode
- Styling: Tailwind CSS
- DB: Prisma + PostgreSQL

fields.label: Additional Instructions
fields.description: Any other preferences, constraints, or context for Copilot to follow across this repository.
fields.placeholder:
- Prefer small, focused changes over large refactors.
- Always add JSDoc to exported functions.
- Do not introduce new dependencies without discussion.

steps.title: Path-Specific Instructions
steps.description: Configure .github/instructions/code.instructions.md — rules applied when Copilot works in files matching a glob pattern.
steps.hint: Path-specific files use an applyTo frontmatter field with comma-separated glob patterns. They are combined with the repo-wide instructions when both match.

fields.label: Apply To (Glob Patterns)
fields.description: Comma-separated glob patterns. These instructions activate when Copilot is editing matching files.

presets.label: Tests only
presets.value: **/*.test.ts,**/*.spec.ts,**/*.test.tsx

presets.label: API routes
presets.value: src/app/api/**,backend/**

fields.label: File-Type Instructions
fields.description: Specific guidance that applies when working in files matching the glob patterns above.
fields.placeholder:
- Use TypeScript strict mode patterns.
- Prefer explicit types at all public API boundaries.
- Use `readonly` for parameters that should not be mutated.
- Avoid type assertions (`as`) unless absolutely necessary.
fields.screen_hint: These instructions are only injected when Copilot is editing files matching your glob pattern. Keep them concise and file-type-specific.

presets.label: TypeScript strict
presets.value:
- Use TypeScript strict mode patterns.
- Prefer explicit types at all public API boundaries.
- Use `readonly` for function parameters that should not be mutated.
- Avoid type assertions (`as`) unless unavoidable.

presets.label: React components
presets.value:
- Use functional components and hooks exclusively. Never use class components.
- Keep components focused on a single concern.
- Extract complex logic into custom hooks.

presets.label: FastAPI routes
presets.value:
- Validate all request inputs using Pydantic models.
- Return consistent error shapes: { detail: string }.
- Use async def for all route handlers.

presets.label: Test files
presets.value:
- Use Arrange / Act / Assert structure.
- Test at the behaviour level, not the implementation level.
- Mock external I/O (HTTP, database, filesystem).

steps.title: Agent Instructions (AGENTS.md)
steps.description: Configure AGENTS.md — guidance for Copilot coding agents and agentic workflows in this repository.
steps.hint: AGENTS.md is read by Copilot coding agents when executing automated tasks. The nearest AGENTS.md in the directory tree takes precedence. Keep content concise and action-oriented.

fields.label: Build & Test Commands
fields.description: The exact commands the agent must use to build, test, and validate this project.
fields.placeholder:
- Install: npm install
- Build: npm run build
- Test: npm test
- Lint: npm run lint

presets.label: npm / TypeScript
presets.value:
- Install: npm install
- Build: npm run build
- Test: npm test
- Lint: npm run lint

presets.label: Python / FastAPI
presets.value:
- Install: pip install -r requirements.txt
- Test: python3 -m pytest
- Lint: python3 -m ruff check .

presets.label: Makefile monorepo
presets.value:
- Install all: make install
- Run both: make dev
- Build frontend: make build

fields.label: Naming & Pattern Conventions
fields.description: File naming conventions, directory structure, and code patterns the agent should follow.
fields.placeholder:
- React components: PascalCase files in src/components/.
- Utility functions: camelCase.
- Test files co-located with source: *.test.ts

presets.label: React project
presets.value:
- Components: PascalCase in src/components/.
- Hooks: camelCase prefixed with `use` in src/hooks/.
- Utilities: camelCase in src/utils/.
- Tests: co-located *.test.ts files.

presets.label: FastAPI project
presets.value:
- Routers in app/routers/, models in app/models/, services in app/services/.
- Pydantic models: PascalCase.
- Route functions: snake_case.

fields.label: Do Not Modify
fields.description: Files or directories the agent must never touch.
fields.locked_value:
- Do not modify generated, vendored, or third-party files.
- Do not edit lock files (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, Pipfile.lock, go.sum) by hand.
- Never commit secrets, API keys, or credentials to source control.

presets.label: Common generated files
presets.value:
- Do not modify files under dist/, build/, or .next/.
- Do not edit package-lock.json or yarn.lock directly.
- Do not modify __pycache__/ or *.pyc files.

fields.label: Additional Agent Guidance
fields.description: Any other constraints, workflows, or preferences the agent should follow.
fields.locked_value:
- Run the full test suite after making changes. Fix all failures before marking a task complete.
- Review every change for security implications before committing.
- Return meaningful error messages. Never expose stack traces or internal paths to end users.
- Commit changes in logical, atomic units with clear commit messages.

presets.label: Safety first
presets.value:
- Always run tests after making changes and fix any failures before marking a task done.
- Commit changes in logical, atomic units.
- Never push to main directly.

steps.title: Repository Settings (.github/copilot/settings.json)
steps.description: Configure repository-level Copilot CLI settings committed for your whole team.
steps.hint: Commit settings.json for team-wide Copilot CLI defaults. The companyAnnouncements key injects a reminder message into all Copilot contexts for this repository.

fields.label: Settings JSON
fields.screen_hint: The companyAnnouncements array injects a short message into all Copilot Chat sessions for this repo — useful for linking to CONTRIBUTING.md or reminding contributors to run tests.

presets.label: Require tests before commit
Link to CONTRIBUTING.md

steps.title: Custom Agent Definitions
steps.description: Define custom Copilot agents with specific roles, tool access, and instructions for agentic workflows.
steps.hint: Custom agent profiles give Copilot a specific persona, tool set, and system prompt for a particular workflow. They are stored as .agent.md files in .github/agents/ and can be invoked by name in Copilot Chat or used by the coding agent on GitHub.com. Repo agents are shared via version control; user agents live in ~/.copilot/agents/.

fields.label: Copilot Agents
fields.description: Define one or more custom agents. Each becomes a .agent.md file inside .github/agents/.
fields.screen_hint: Each agent gets a dedicated system prompt and a restricted set of tools. Narrow the tool list to the minimum needed for the agent's role — this makes agents safer and more focused. The name field becomes the filename and the @-mention handle in Copilot Chat.

presets.label: Security Reviewer
presets.description: Reviews code changes for security issues

value.name: security-reviewer
value.description: Reviews code changes for security vulnerabilities and suggests actionable fixes
value.system_prompt:
Review the provided code changes for security vulnerabilities including injection flaws, broken authentication, sensitive data exposure, missing authorisation checks, and insecure dependencies.

For each issue: state the severity, describe the problem, and provide a concrete fix. Focus only on security — not style or architecture.

presets.label: PR Reviewer
presets.description: Reviews pull requests for quality and correctness

value.name: pr-reviewer
value.description: Reviews pull requests for correctness, consistency, and maintainability
value.system_prompt: Perform a thorough code review of the provided changes as a senior engineer. Cover: correctness, logic errors, edge cases, consistency with existing patterns, missing error handling, and performance red flags. Be constructive — for each issue explain why it matters and how to fix it.

presets.label: Test Writer
presets.description: Writes tests for new or changed code

value.name: test-writer
value.description: Writes unit and integration tests for new or changed code using the project's existing test framework
value.system_prompt: Write tests for the code you are given. Use the testing framework already present in the project. Cover happy paths, edge cases, and error conditions. Keep each test focused on one behaviour. Run the tests after writing them and fix any failures.

presets.label: Documentation Writer
presets.description: Writes inline docs and README sections

value.name: documentation-writer
value.description: Writes and updates inline documentation and README content following project conventions
value.system_prompt: Write and update documentation for the provided code. Match the documentation style already used in the project. Document all public APIs with a summary, parameters, return values, and usage examples where helpful. Do not modify source logic.
